frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment:
Ashok: I had spoken to that lady once or twice, but she kept being rather cold. Later I came to know she is already committed, so naturally I stepped back—one should respect that. 
Batchmate: Hmm. 
Ashok: If she had just said that clearly in the first place, there would have been no awkwardness.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because he frames the woman's being 'already committed' as the real reason to stop, rather than respecting her prior disinterest. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring a woman's refusal/coldness until a male attachment is known, presented as polite respectability.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
